Input.Title: Whole-brain annotation and multi-connectome cell typing quantifies circuit stereotypy in Drosophila
Input.Abstract: The fruit fly Drosophila melanogaster combines sophisticated behaviour with tractable nervous system...

Problem: Investigating the Functional Implications of Connectome Variability in Drosophila’s Learning and Memory Circuits Across Different Environmental and Genetic Contexts
Method: Multi-tiered approach integrates connectomics, behavioral assays, genetic manipulation CRISPR-Cas9 PINK1 Parkin, transcriptomic single-cell RNA sequencing spatial transcriptomics, computational modeling network analysis simulation of variability
Experiment: 7 steps: Pre-experimental Setup breeding program standardized rearing CRISPR constructs training; Connectome Mapping Variability Analysis random assignment control/treatment high-resolution imaging mushroom body ML quantification; Behavioral Assays olfactory conditioning visual pattern recognition baseline; Environmental Genetic Perturbations varied learning paradigms sensory inputs gene editing; Transcriptomic Spatial Analysis scRNA sequencing spatial transcriptomics correlate; Computational Modeling Network Analysis simulate information flow olfactory projection; Synthesis Validation iterative experimentation refinement
